LAW AND MOTION 302, RE: DEFENDANT BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE COUNSEL AND COMPEL TRANSFER OF FILE MATERIALS. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT'S UNOPPOSED "MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE COUNSEL AND COMPEL TRANSFER OF FILE MATERIALS" IS GRANTED. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER JR. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/RBU)

CRC 3.1385 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jan-10-2024 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: No appearance. Notice having been given and there being no appearance or objection filed, the Court orders the case dismissed pursuant to California Rules of Court Section 3.1385. Judge: Anne-Christine Massullo; Clerk: S. Le; Proceedings not reported. (610)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Nov-01-2023 Case Settled - Set For Crc 3.1385 Hrg On Jan-09-24 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Motion To Substitute Counsel And Compel Transfer Of File Materials
Off Calendar Jul-17-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant BAUER'S INTELLIGENT TRANSPORTATION, INC.'s Motion to Substitute Counsel And Compel Transfer Of File Materials.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person.

The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant's unopposed "motion to substitute counsel and compel transfer of file materials" is granted.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not reported. (302/RBU)
